{"query":"Notre-Dame Paris South Tower restoration completed belfry beams roof 2025 status","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1139,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.friendsofnotredamedeparis.org/notre-dame-cathedrals-2025-reconstruction-progress-updates/","title":"Notre-Dame Cathedral's 2025 Reconstruction Progress Updates | Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris","content":"Paused in 2024 to allow the vaults to be closed, the roofing of the lower part of the spire still needed to be completed.
In June and July 2025, it is the turn of the 16 statues of the apostles to return to their place at the foot of the spire, after their restoration in the workshop.
On July 17, Emmanuel and Marie made their sounds heard in the Parisian sky for their first flight, which allowed them to demonstrate their proper functioning and to make final adjustments."},{"url":"https://www.rfi.fr/en/france/20250920-notre-dame-de-paris-towers-to-reopen-to-public-in-time-for-eu-heritage-days","title":"Notre-Dame cathedral towers reopen for first time in six years - RFI","content":"From Saturday, visitors can once again climb more than 400 steps to the top of the towers of Notre-Dame de Paris, which have been fully restored after the fire that ravaged the cathedral in 2019.
While the body of Notre-Dame cathedral reopened to the public in December 2024, the iconic twin towers of Notre-Dame cathedral have been closed since the devastating fire of 15 April 2019.
They reopened to the public on Saturday, coinciding with the annual European Heritage Days – a continent-wide event that sees historic monuments, companies and workshops open their doors to the public.
A maximum of 19 people can enter at a time.
Since Notre-Dame de Paris reopened on 7 and 8 December last year, it has recorded more than eight million visitors, becoming "the most visited monument in France", according to the president's office."},{"url":"https://thegoodnewsroom.org/notre-dame-prepares-to-reopen-towers-tour-with-return-of-famed-statues-to-rooftop/","title":"Notre Dame Prepares To Reopen Towers' Tour With Return of Famed Statues to Rooftop - The Good Newsroom","content":"PARIS (OSV News) — Symbolic lifting of the statues surrounding the spire of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris marked the beginning of one of the last phases of the roof reconstruction before the towers of the Parisian icon will reopen to the public on September 20.
The restoration work lasted from April 2019 to June 2021 in the Dordogne region of southern France, near Bordeaux.
Costing $1.64 million, 85% of the restoration costs were funded by the Fondation Notre Dame, chaired by the archbishop of Paris.
“This work had already been planned before the fire and should have been undertaken in 2019,” Father Ribadeau Dumas, rector of the cathedral, told OSV News.
Another project will involve replacing the discreet, non-figurative stained-glass windows that Viollet-le-Duc installed in six side chapels on the south side of the cathedral — with contemporary figurative stained-glass windows on the theme of Pentecost, a project expected to be completed by December 2026."},{"url":"https://www.goodmorningamerica.com/news/story/france-officially-reopens-notre-dames-cathedral-towers-6-125726918","title":"France officially reopens Notre Dame's cathedral towers 6 years after devastating fire - Good Morning America","content":"LONDON -- French President Emmanuel Macron has climbed the steps to the belfry of Notre Dame to officially reopen the north and south towers of the cathedral nearly six and half years after a fire destroyed the building in 2019.
7, 2024, to attend the ceremonial reopening of the Paris cathedral in France five years after flames engulfed the historic site."}]}
